name: Pamela's PRO Workout
description: Compact programmable clock-synced modulation source with 8 independent outputs. Each output freely configurable as LFO, trigger, envelope, Euclidean pattern, or random. BPM 10–330, with CV inputs and cross-output modulation.

controls.name: Program Knob
controls.description: Push-turn encoder for all UI navigation. Twist to scroll outputs/values, push to select, push+hold to access extended parameters.
controls.detail: Push: select · Twist: adjust · Hold (>1s): extended params

controls.name: Start / Stop Button
controls.description: Starts and stops the master clock. Pressing stop also resets clock position.

inputs.name: CLK
inputs.description: External clock input. Overrides internal BPM when patched. Rising edge 700mV min.

inputs.name: RUN
inputs.description: Run/stop input. High voltage starts clock, low stops it. Can also act as CV 3 or Reset.

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: Assignable CV input 1. Assignable to any output parameter with individual attenuation and offset.

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: Assignable CV input 2. Assignable to any output parameter with individual attenuation and offset.

outputs.name: OUT 1–8
outputs.description: Eight independent clocked CV/gate outputs with LED indicators. Each fully programmable with shape, divisor/multiplier, Euclidean, probability, and cross-modulation.

behaviors.name: Per-Output Clock Division / Multiplication
behaviors.description: Each output has an independent multiplier or divisor from /16384 to ×192 including non-integer triplet and dotted values. Utility modes: GATE (held high), OFF, START pulse, STOP pulse.

behaviors.name: Waveform Shapes
behaviors.description: Each output selects from: Gate/Pulse, Ratchet ×2/×4, Triangle, Trapezoid, Sine, Hump, Exp/Log Envelope, Classic Random (S&H), Smooth Random. Width/Slew parameter shapes each waveform.

behaviors.name: Euclidean Patterns
behaviors.description: Generate Euclidean rhythms per output. Set Steps (total), Triggers (hits), Pad (empty suffix steps), and Shift (rotation). Pattern loops can reset non-4/4 patterns.

behaviors.name: Probability & Loops
behaviors.description: Probability sets % chance each step fires. Loop specifies reset point in beats. Loop Nap/Wake creates sleeping patterns (output off for N loops, active for M loops).

behaviors.name: Cross Operations
behaviors.description: 17 operations between outputs: AND, OR, S&H, MULT, MASK, SEED, FLIP, MIN, MAX, and more. Enables internal modulation routing without cables.

behaviors.name: FLEX Timing
behaviors.description: Micro-timing offsets: HUMAN (random drift), SWING (every-other delay), RAMP UP/DOWN (accelerating/decelerating), HUMP (arc tempo).

behaviors.name: Loop Wake/Nap
behaviors.description: Output plays for N loops (Wake), then silences for N loops (Nap). Creates call-and-response phrases and automatic fills without any external sequencer or logic.

behaviors.name: Quantiser
behaviors.description: Quantises output to musical scales. Custom scales saveable. Applied after cross operations.

behaviors.name: Triggered envelope mode
behaviors.description: Setting an output to Triggered type converts any waveform shape into a one-shot envelope that fires on each rising edge from a CV input. Pamela becomes an 8-channel clocked envelope generator — each channel can have a different shape (exp, log, sine, triangle) and length independent of the master BPM.

behaviors.name: Loop Shift for evolution
behaviors.description: Loop Shift advances the start point of a pattern by N steps each time the loop resets. The pattern slowly walks through a longer underlying sequence cycle by cycle, preventing static repetition over long periods. Combine with Euclidean patterns for sequences that feel generative without true randomness.

behaviors.name: RESEED for controlled randomness
behaviors.description: One output set to Cross Op SEED with Cross Src pointing to another output: the source output's rising edge resets the random seed of the SEED output, causing it to replay the same random sequence from that reset point. Provides deterministic re-rolls on demand — useful for generative patches that need repeatability at phrase boundaries.

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean clave
patchIdeas.patch: Steps=16 · Triggers=7 · Rotate=0 · classic bell pattern · Rotate by 1-2 for Cuban feel
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=7 Rotate=0

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // classic clave bell pattern

patchIdeas.name: Internal S&H sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: Smooth Random · Loop=16 · Out B: gate /4 · Out A Cross Op: S&H · Cross Src: Out B · stepped random CV locked to quarter notes · patch Out A to oscillator V/oct for a clocked random melodic sequence with no external S&H module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Smooth Random, Loop=16, Cross Op S&H, Cross Src OUT 2
* OUT 2: Gate /4

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/oct) // stepped random melody on quarter notes

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythm with prime divisions
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: /7 gate · Out B: /11 gate · Out C: /13 gate · LCM = 1001 steps before pattern repeats · run all three to separate drum voices or oscillator triggers · near-infinite rhythmic variation for generative ambient without any randomness
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /7
* OUT 2: Gate /11
* OUT 3: Gate /13

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Drum 1 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Drum 2 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) g> Drum 3 (Trig) // LCM 1001 steps before repeat

patchIdeas.name: Gate masking for ghost notes
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=13 (dense pattern) · Out B: euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=5 (sparse pattern) · Out A Cross Op: MASK · Cross Src: Out B · Out A triggers only fire where Out B pattern allows · dense rhythm filtered down to sparse, syncopated ghost hits
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/13, Cross Op MASK, Cross Src OUT 2
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // dense pattern masked to sparse ghost hits

patchIdeas.name: Boolean rhythm logic
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=7 Rotate=0 · Out B: euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=5 Rotate=3 · Out C: gate /1 · Out C Cross Op: AND · Cross Src: Out A · Out C fires only where both Out A and Out B coincide · sparse, syncopated intersections that shift with rotations
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/7 Rotate=0
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5 Rotate=3
* OUT 3: Gate /1, Cross Op AND, Cross Src OUT 1

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Drum (Trig) // fires only where OUT 1 AND OUT 2 coincide

patchIdeas.name: Wake/Nap call-and-response
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: melody CV source · Loop=4 · Wake=2 · Nap=1 · Out A plays two phrases then rests for one loop · Out B answers in the gap: Loop=4 · Wake=1 · Nap=2 · plays only while Out A rests · natural musical breathing and dialogue between two voices with no manual muting
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Melody CV, Loop=4, Wake=2, Nap=1
* OUT 2: Melody CV, Loop=4, Wake=1, Nap=2

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/oct) // call
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/oct) // response in the gap

patchIdeas.name: Drum fill every 8 bars
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: main drum trigger running continuously · Out B: fill trigger (Steps=16 Triggers=12) · Out B Loop=8 · Wake=1 · Nap=7 · Out B is silent for 7 bars then fires on bar 8 only · automatic periodic fills locked to musical phrase length
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Main drum trigger, continuous
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/12, Loop=8, Wake=1, Nap=7

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Fill Drum (Trig) // fires only on bar 8

patchIdeas.name: Smooth random portamento
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: Smooth Random · Rate multiplier ×4 · Loop=8 · Quantize=pentatonic · Out A → oscillator V/oct · gliding voltage snaps to scale degrees with portamento preserved between quantize steps · melodic random with no harsh jumps
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Smooth Random, rate x4, Loop=8, Quantize=pentatonic

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/oct) // gliding pentatonic melody

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature sine pair
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: sine LFO · rate /4 · Out B: sine LFO same rate · Phase=90 on Out B · patch Out A and Out B to X/Y inputs of a Lissajous-capable scope or to two VCA/filter parameters · creates circular modulation paths, rotating stereo effects, or smooth 2D parameter sweeps
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Sine LFO /4
* OUT 2: Sine LFO /4, Phase=90

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) >> VCA L (CV)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) >> VCA R (CV) // quadrature rotating stereo

patchIdeas.name: FLEX humanized hi-hat
patchIdeas.patch: Out A: euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=12 · FLEX Op: HUMAN · FLEX Amount: 15% · patch Out A to closed hi-hat trigger · subtle random micro-timing drift on every hit · sounds like a real drummer playing a repetitive pattern rather than a machine — increase Amount to 30-40% for obvious drunk-drummer feel
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/12, FLEX HUMAN 15%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Hi-Hat (Trig) // micro-timing drift per hit

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean polyrhythm engine (3:4:5:7)
patchIdeas.patch: Four interlocking euclidean patterns on four outputs · OUT 1: E(3,16) → kick · OUT 2: E(5,16) rotate+2 → snare · OUT 3: E(7,16) rotate+5 → hat · OUT 4: E(4,16) rotate+3 → perc · different trigger counts and rotations produce an evolving polyrhythmic groove that only resolves after hundreds of bars — a complete drum bed from one module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/3
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5 Rotate=2
* OUT 3: Euclidean 16/7 Rotate=5
* OUT 4: Euclidean 16/4 Rotate=3

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) t> Perc (Trig) // resolves every ~420 steps

patchIdeas.name: Self-clocking probability cascade
patchIdeas.patch: Slow output restarts Pam's itself · OUT 8: slowest division with EOC mode → patch into Pam's RUN input · OUT 1-4 with descending probability (100%, 75%, 50%, 25%) driving drum voices · each phrase restart re-rolls which steps fire, producing aleatoric phrase-to-phrase variation without any external randomness source
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4, p=100%
* OUT 2: Gate /4, p=75%
* OUT 3: Gate /4, p=50%
* OUT 4: Gate /4, p=25%
* OUT 8: /64, EOC pulse

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Pamela's PRO Workout (RUN) // self-reset for aleatoric phrases
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) t> Perc (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Slow LFO envelope generator
patchIdeas.patch: Tempo-synced filter and amp swells using sine and ramp shapes · OUT 5: sine, /1 bar, Phase=90 → VCF cutoff · OUT 6: saw-down, /2 bars → VCA level · phase offset creates drifting filter bloom followed by amp decay · fully BPM-locked evolving pad motion with no dedicated LFO or envelope modules
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 5: Sine LFO /1 bar, Phase=90
* OUT 6: Saw Down /2 bars

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> VCA (Level) // tempo-synced pad swells

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean chord arpeggiator
patchIdeas.patch: Gate and quantized CV share a division to create a melodic arp · OUT 1: E(5,8) triggers → envelope · OUT 2: S&H random, quantized A minor, /8, new-CV-on-trig-only → VCO V/oct · each euclidean hit simultaneously fires the envelope and advances a new scale-locked pitch · instant euclidean arpeggiator from one module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 8/5 Trig
* OUT 2: S&H /8, Quantize=A minor, new-CV-on-trig

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO (V/oct) // euclidean arp in A minor
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: Three prime divisions merged via external OR logic for drum-fill chaos · OUT 1 /3, OUT 2 /5, OUT 3 /7 · all set to 2ms width · patch through logic OR to single percussion trigger · global FLEX swing 15% for human feel · dense, unpredictable rolls that never repeat identically within a phrase
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /3, width 2ms
* OUT 2: Gate /5, width 2ms
* OUT 3: Gate /7, width 2ms

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Logic OR (A)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Logic OR (B)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) g> Logic OR (C)
- Logic OR (Out) t> Percussion (Trig) // prime-division burst fills

patchIdeas.name: Evolving generative brain
patchIdeas.patch: All 8 outputs orchestrating a full patch from one module · OUT 1: clock → external sequencer · OUT 2: gate → envelope · OUT 3: quantized CV → pitch · OUT 4: S&H → timbre · OUT 5: sine LFO → filter · OUT 6: ramp → amp swell · OUT 7: E(2,16) → reverb send · OUT 8: /64 pulse → scene/reset · Pam's becomes a complete generative conductor with clocks, gates, pitches, modulators and scene changes
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Clock /16
* OUT 2: Gate /4
* OUT 3: S&H Quantized
* OUT 4: S&H Smooth
* OUT 5: Sine LFO /1
* OUT 6: Ramp /2
* OUT 7: Euclidean 16/2
* OUT 8: /64 pulse

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Sequencer (Clock)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) >> VCO (Timbre)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> VCA (Level)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 7) g> Reverb (Send Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Sequencer (Reset) // full patch brain

patchIdeas.name: Probability-swapped hi-hats
patchIdeas.patch: Same division with inverse probabilities produces natural closed/open hat variation · OUT 1 /16 probability 80% swing +8% → closed hat · OUT 2 /16 probability 20% swing 0% → open hat · the two rarely overlap and the differing swing adds groove · classic hat pattern with no programming
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /16, p=80%, FLEX SWING +8%
* OUT 2: Gate /16, p=20%, FLEX SWING 0%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Closed Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Open Hat (Trig) // inverse probability variation

patchIdeas.name: Phase-offset dual LFO FM+pan
patchIdeas.patch: Quadrature sines drive FM index and stereo pan for rotating motion · OUT 5 sine /1 bar phase 0° → VCO FM index · OUT 6 sine /1 bar phase 90° → stereo pan CV · the 90° offset makes spectral brightness peak as the sound crosses center · organic rotating timbre in stereo field
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 5: Sine LFO /1 bar, Phase=0
* OUT 6: Sine LFO /1 bar, Phase=90

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCO (FM Index)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> Panner (CV) // 90° offset brightness vs position

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean rotation performance
patchIdeas.patch: Live pattern mutation via CV rotation · external slow LFO or disting → Pam's CV 1 input · CV 1 assigned to OUT 1 Rotation parameter · OUT 1: E(5,16) → drum trigger · slowly sweep the CV to walk the pattern through all 16 possible rotations · performance-friendly live mutation without touching the encoder
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/5, CV1 → Rotation

- External LFO (Out) >> Pamela's PRO Workout (CV 1)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // CV sweeps through all 16 rotations

patchIdeas.name: Clock-divided ratcheting kick
patchIdeas.patch: Probability on fast division creates rolls on main beats · OUT 1 /4 probability 100% → main kick · OUT 2 /32 probability 25% → ratchet voice · OR the two externally to the kick trigger · main downbeats always land, with sparse high-speed ratchets filling between them for techno-flavored momentum
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4, p=100%
* OUT 2: Gate /32, p=25%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Logic OR (A)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Logic OR (B)
- Logic OR (Out) t> Kick (Trig) // main beats with random ratchets

patchIdeas.name: Two-against-three melodic counterpoint
patchIdeas.patch: Two voices on different divisions create natural counterpoint · OUT 1 /4 trig + OUT 2 /4 quantized CV → Voice A (env + pitch) · OUT 3 /6 trig + OUT 4 /6 quantized CV → Voice B (env + pitch) · same scale on both, different octave ranges · 4-against-6 polymetric phrasing produces convergence and divergence without any sequencer programming
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4
* OUT 2: S&H /4, Quantize=D dorian (low range)
* OUT 3: Gate /6
* OUT 4: S&H /6, Quantize=D dorian (high range)

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Env A (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO A (V/oct)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) g> Env B (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) p> VCO B (V/oct) // 4-against-6 counterpoint

patchIdeas.name: EOR scene trigger chain
patchIdeas.patch: One output marks end-of-phrase and advances multiple modules at once · OUT 8: E(1,64) rotate=63 width 20ms · fires exactly one pulse at the end of every 64-step phrase · fan out to Marbles t-reset, envelope stages reset, and filter-bank scene advance · automatic phrase-synced scene progression for long-form generative pieces
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 8: Euclidean 64/1 Rotate=63, width 20ms

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Marbles (t Reset)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Stages (Reset)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Filter Bank (Scene+) // end-of-phrase scene advance

patchIdeas.name: Multi-envelope rhythm bed (hub-and-spoke)
patchIdeas.patch: Pam's as the clock brain for an entire drum voice bed · OUT 1 /4 → Env 1 → Kick VCA · OUT 2 /16 probability 60% → Env 2 → Hat VCA · OUT 3 euclidean 16/5 → Env 3 → Perc VCA · OUT 4 sine LFO /2 bars → mixer group level for breathing dynamics · one Pam's drives three separate envelope+VCA paths plus a slow level animator, giving a full rhythm section from a single clock source.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4
* OUT 2: Gate /16, p=60%
* OUT 3: Euclidean 16/5
* OUT 4: Sine LFO /2 bars

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Env 1 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Env 2 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Env 3 (Trig)
- Env 1 (Out) >> VCA Kick (CV)
- Env 2 (Out) >> VCA Hat (CV)
- Env 3 (Out) >> VCA Perc (CV)
- Kick VCO (Out) -> VCA Kick (In) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Hat Noise (Out) -> VCA Hat (In) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Perc Src (Out) -> VCA Perc (In) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) >> Mixer (Level CV) // breathing dynamics
